name: Pivot Circle
tagline: Find your people before the moment passes.
description: Pivot Circle is a social networking app that connects people in the exact in-between moments of daily life, such as after a canceled plan, during a solo lunch break, or when moving to a new neighborhood. Instead of following influencers or browsing endless feeds, users post short-lived "pivots" that describe what they suddenly have free time for, what kind of company they want, and how far they’re willing to travel. The app then matches them with nearby people whose timing, mood, and preferences align, making it easy to turn dead time into meaningful micro-connections. It solves the common problem of loneliness and logistical friction by making spontaneous socializing feel safe, structured, and low-pressure. For example, someone stuck downtown after a meeting can find a nearby person also free for a 20-minute coffee, or a new parent can discover another parent available for a stroller walk in the park. What makes Pivot Circle unique is that it prioritizes moment-to-moment compatibility over identity curation, creating a social network built around actual availability and intent rather than polished profiles. Over time, it helps users build a real-world social fabric from the small openings in their day.
features: The app lets users create time-boxed availability posts called pivots, so people can signal exactly when they are free without committing to long plans.
It matches users based on location, schedule overlap, activity preference, and social energy level, which makes spontaneous meetups feel easier and safer.
It includes lightweight safety controls such as mutual verification, visible meeting zones, and auto-expiring chats, which help users meet strangers with confidence.
It offers context-based prompts like "want a walk, coffee, or quiet coworking?" so users can quickly decide how to turn free time into a real connection.
It tracks repeated positive interactions to suggest recurring micro-communities, helping casual one-off meetups grow into lasting friendships.

Urban professionals, remote workers, recent movers, students, and parents aged roughly 20-45 who often have fragmented schedules and want more natural ways to meet people nearby. It is especially useful for people who are socially open but dislike the effort, uncertainty, and awkwardness of traditional event-based networking.

useCases":["Sarah just finished a stressful meeting and has 40 minutes before her train, so she opens Pivot Circle and finds another nearby user also looking for a quick coffee and decompressing conversation.
